evals.prompt: Upgrade SpecKit in this repo from 0.6.1 to the latest. I haven't customized anything; just want the new templates.
evals.expected_output: Snapshots the repo, runs the safe (no --force) `specify integration upgrade` for each installed integration, and reports the new manifest hashes. No restore step needed because nothing was modified.
expectations: Snapshots .specify/ (and .claude/, .codex/, .github/ as applicable) to /tmp/specify-upgrade-backup-<STAMP>/ BEFORE running any upgrade
Runs `specify integration upgrade <key>` without --force on the first attempt for each installed integration
Surfaces the new CLI version and manifest hash deltas
Surfaces the backup path explicitly so the operator knows where to roll back from

evals.prompt: Upgrade SpecKit but I've heavily customized .specify/memory/constitution.md and a few template files — don't lose my customizations.
evals.expected_output: Snapshots before any mutation. First-pass safe upgrade gets blocked by the CLI on the modified files. Skill surfaces the blocked-files list, asks the operator to choose force-and-restore / keep-mine / manual-merge. On force-and-restore: runs --force, then restores constitution.md from the backup verbatim and offers file-by-file diff review for the other modified files.
expectations: Always snapshots first to /tmp/specify-upgrade-backup-<STAMP>/
Tries `specify integration upgrade <key>` WITHOUT --force first
Detects the diff-aware block, surfaces the modified-files list to the operator
Asks for explicit choice between force-and-restore, keep-mine, or manual-merge
On force-and-restore: runs `specify integration upgrade <key> --force`, then restores constitution.md from the backup verbatim
For non-constitution modified files: shows the diff and asks the operator whether to restore the backup or keep the upgrade's new version

evals.prompt: I'm upgrading from spec-kit 0.5 (slash-command mode) to 0.8.13 (skills mode). Both .claude/commands/speckit.*.md and the new .claude/skills/speckit-*/ exist now — clean up the duplicates.
evals.expected_output: Detects both legacy slash-command files and new skills directories. Offers to dedupe the legacy speckit.*.md files. Hard-guarantees to preserve extension commands (speckit.speckit-utils.*.md) and any non-speckit-prefixed commands. Shows the exact deletion list before running rm.
expectations: Lists both `.claude/commands/speckit.*.md` and `.claude/skills/speckit-*/SKILL.md` to detect the duplicate state
Repeats the check for the Codex side (`.codex/prompts/speckit.*.md` vs `.codex/skills/speckit-*/SKILL.md`)
Offers the operator a dedupe choice with `keep-both` as an option
When deduping: only deletes files matching `speckit.<single-word>.md` (e.g., speckit.constitution.md, speckit.specify.md)
Explicitly preserves `speckit.speckit-utils.*.md` files (these are extension commands, not SpecKit-managed)
Explicitly preserves any non-`speckit.`-prefixed commands
Shows the exact deletion list to the operator before running rm

evals.prompt: Upgrade SpecKit in this repo — but there is no .specify/ directory.
evals.expected_output: Detects the ABSENT state and hands off to $speckit-install rather than attempting an upgrade that has nothing to upgrade.
expectations: Checks `test -d .specify` and detects ABSENT
Does NOT attempt to run `specify integration upgrade` (would fail)
Hands off to `$speckit-install` with a clear explanation that upgrade only operates on existing installs
